strategy: Team fight at 34,000 health: run one hitter, supports who feed blades late, and a healer who watches Power Play and Scald.
He resists every school 39% and has no boost school, so hit with your highest-damage school or a Spectral hit and burst through in one shot.
Every Blade a Wizard casts makes Belloq Balanceblade himself, buffing his own attacks. Keep blades on the hitter only and stack them in the last setup round.
Every Trap a Wizard casts drops an Elemental Trap on that caster. Minimize traps or cleanse them off.
Expect Power Play on the opening global and plan pip economy around the conversion. Clear Scald with Triage if it lands.
cheatNotes: He opens with Power Play, converting normal pips to power pips. Plan your pip usage around the shift.
Casting a Trap makes him drop an Elemental Trap on you, the caster. Avoid trap stacking or expect the counter.
Every Blade you cast gives him a Balanceblade, buffing his own hits. Keep blades on the hitter only and stack them late.
He casts Scald, a Fire damage-over-time, on an unknown trigger. Clear it with Triage.
